what_is_interesting: Marcus Aurelius wrote the Meditations as private, unguarded notes to himself — never meant for anyone else — and 2,000 years later, those intimate thoughts of a Roman emperor are the most effective tool for quieting the modern, anxious mind to sleep.
likely_image: A marble bust or statue of Marcus Aurelius against a dark, starlit or candlelit background — calm, ancient, slightly ethereal.
winner_reasoning: With a Marcus Aurelius bust staring out of the dark, 'STILL WHISPERING' triggers the same present-tense dread-and-wonder as 'STILL KILLING' — his voice is 2,000 years dead and yet it reaches you tonight, in the dark, right now, making the viewer feel the strange intimacy of ancient words designed to put a modern mind to sleep.
